artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery found Hiroshima University's 23 May 2023 policy on generative AI in education in Japanese and an official English mirror. A Hiroshima University media-center survey page was logged as limited context and rejected as a primary policy source because it reports survey results and explicitly points back to the education policy.

artifacts.claimText: Hiroshima University has an official 23 May 2023 page setting out its policy on use of generative AI, including ChatGPT, in educational activities.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AI（ChatGPT等）の利用について - 学びのサポート - 広島大学
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Hiroshima University's basic policy frames student use of generative AI in coursework around students identifying problems and considering solutions themselves, while users understand AI's mechanisms, benefits, risks, and changing literacy requirements.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AI（ChatGPT等）の利用について - 学びのサポート - 広島大学
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Hiroshima University tells course and thesis supervisors to judge whether generative AI may be used in classes or assignments and explain that decision to students through syllabi, guidance, or similar channels.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AI（ChatGPT等）の利用について - 学びのサポート - 広島大学
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Where generative AI use is allowed, Hiroshima University's policy says students should be told that using AI output as-is may amount to copyright infringement or plagiarism, and that any use or quoted output should be made clear in reports and similar assignments.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AI（ChatGPT等）の利用について - 学びのサポート - 広島大学
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Where generative AI use is allowed, Hiroshima University's policy says students should be warned that AI use can lead to leakage of personal or confidential information and that generated outputs may be incorrect.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AI（ChatGPT等）の利用について - 学びのサポート - 広島大学
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Hiroshima University's policy says teaching staff should revise teaching methods, content, assignments, and evaluation criteria when needed and evaluate student performance appropriately in light of generative AI use.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 大学での学びは、自らで課題を発見し、自分自身で解決策を考え、そして行動することが基本であり、学生が授業においてChatGPT等の生成AIをレポート等の課題作成に利用する場合には、このことを念頭に置く必要がある。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: University learning is based on students identifying problems and considering solutions themselves; students using ChatGPT or similar generative AI for assignments should keep that in mind.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal:
授業担当教員や指導教員は、以下の点に留意して授業等を実施する。

1) 授業内やレポート等の課題作成における利用の可否について適切に判断し、シラバスやガイダンス等で学生へ説明する。

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 利用を可とする場合は、学生に対して、生成AIの出力結果をそのまま利用することが著作権侵害や剽窃にあたる可能性があること、個人情報や機密情報の漏洩につながること、出力結果が誤っている可能性があるなどの留意点を十分に伝え、細心の注意を払って利用するよう指導する。

artifacts.notes: Approved as a staged candidate only; the claim is limited to source availability and does not publish a canonical public fact.
Approved as a narrow summary of the basic policy section; it avoids saying all AI use is generally permitted.
Approved as course-level discretion and communication guidance, not a blanket university-wide permission rule.
Approved as a warning/disclosure claim tied directly to Section 3 language.
Approved as a privacy-risk warning claim; it does not infer tool procurement or security-review requirements.
Approved as a teaching and assessment review claim limited to the policy's Section 3 item 4 wording.

artifacts.title: Hiroshima University Generative AI Education Policy Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official Hiroshima University sources show a 23 May 2023 policy for generative AI in educational activities. The staged claims cover source status, course-level permission communication, academic integrity and privacy cautions, and assessment review guidance. A media-center survey page was rejected as a primary policy source because it reports survey results and points readers back to the education policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source is an education policy page; this run does not infer broader research, procurement, or institutional security-review rules beyond the cited text.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
